narrative: Today's feed leans heavily on climate and animal-movement findings. In fire ecology, a quantitative framework mapped boreal forest age across the biome, showing bimodal age structures driven by fire and logging regimes, while a zoogeochemistry framework argues animal-driven feedbacks deserve a bigger place in biogeochemical cycling models. Climate coverage ranged from a hierarchical Bayesian model showing strategic dam management can offset warming-driven declines in Yellowstone cutthroat trout productivity, to evidence that Chinook salmon nursery-rearing phenology shifts with warming without truncating development. Notable movement and behaviour work included free-ranging lizards actively regulating ultraviolet exposure independent of thermoregulation, and quolls and Tasmanian devils acting as secondary long-distance dispersers of mycorrhizal fungal spores through mycophagous prey. On invasion, reduced litter allelochemicals were shown to promote positive interactions among alien plant species, supporting the invasional meltdown hypothesis, while a global assessment quantified the ecological footprint of freshwater fish invasions. In landscape ecology, regenerating clearcuts in British Columbia showed signs of functioning as ecological traps for woodland caribou, elevating predation risk despite forage gains. On methods, a large evidence-base review found conservation study designs have shifted markedly toward more robust before-after-control-impact and randomised approaches since 1970, though usage remains uneven across languages and income levels.

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: Introduces a new integrated framework combining eDNA inventories, graphlet network analysis and niche modelling.

methods_radar.title: Defaunation Maps for Tropical Mammals and Birds
methods_radar.note: Introduces a novel modelling framework linking hunting pressure to wildlife population impacts to map pantropical defaunation.

methods_radar.title: Not single large, not several small: the Requirement Estimation Based on Species Ecological Needs (REBSEN) is the best conceptual model to define conservation strategies in severely fragmented landscapes
methods_radar.note: Proposes REBSEN, a new species-needs-based conceptual model replacing the SLOSS framework for fragmented landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Automated mapping of dry stone walls in terraced landscapes using airborne laser scanning data
methods_radar.note: Introduces an automated ALS-based deep learning pipeline for mapping dry stone walls, a previously uninventoried landscape feature.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: Introduces a novel model integrating passive acoustic monitoring with point-count data to estimate absolute density.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: Introduces BIMr2, a new Bayesian tool for inferring migration rates and their environmental drivers from multilocus genotypes.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: Introduces ForestForTrees, a new R package that simplifies Bayesian inference of forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing.

methods_radar.title: sampley: A Python package for sampling visual survey data
methods_radar.note: Introduces sampley, a new Python package standardising spatiotemporal sampling of visual survey transect data.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Develops and benchmarks a species distribution model framework for marine conservation, tested on loggerhead turtles.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Extends camera-trap monitoring vertically from ground to canopy to reveal hidden strata-structured mammal communities.

methods_radar.title: Testing the Influence of Internal Stem Decay and Topography on Biomass and Carbon Stock Estimates in Mongolia’s Boreal Forest
methods_radar.note: Advances aboveground biomass estimation by incorporating internal stem decay and topography into allometric regressions.

methods_radar.title: Temporal and spatial trends in study designs used to understand what works in conservation
methods_radar.note: Evaluates and benchmarks the robustness of conservation study designs used across 8,500+ studies since 1970.

methods_radar.title: Enhancing rule-based potential connectivity models for African savanna elephants (Loxodonta africana) using remote sensing in Binga District, Zimbabwe
methods_radar.note: Enhances rule-based connectivity resistance models for elephants using higher-resolution remote-sensing land-cover data.

methods_radar.title: Discovering the unseen: A performance comparison of taxonomic classification methods for unknown DNA barcodes
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks the performance of taxonomic classification methods for DNA barcodes lacking reference database matches.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Advances transparency in eDNA methodology by systematically addressing false positive and false negative detection errors.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: Advances eDNA methodology to simultaneously characterise community composition and genetic variation together.

methods_radar.title: Detailed maps of European wetlands reveal overlooked patches of ecosystems
methods_radar.note: Advances wetland mapping accuracy by applying a machine-learning algorithm to satellite imagery at high resolution.

methods_radar.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
methods_radar.note: Applies a random forest model combining multisource datasets to map forest age across the boreal biome.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy models to multi-year wolf presence data to assess colonisation and persistence in the Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Applies standardized camera-trap survey data to test the abundant-center hypothesis across ground-dwelling species in China.

methods_radar.title: Is There Mush-room for Bettongs on Dirk Hartog Island? Environmental DNA Assessment of Food Resource Availability for Fauna Translocations
methods_radar.note: Applies environmental DNA metabarcoding to assess fungal food resources ahead of marsupial reintroductions.

methods_radar.title: Topography is a Major Determinant of Forest–Savanna Distributions in Mosaic Landscapes in Central Africa
methods_radar.note: Applies machine learning to topographic variables to predict forest-savanna vegetation state in mosaic landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-source remote sensing data and machine learning to estimate forest soil organic carbon density.

methods_radar.title: From Deficiency to Enrichment: Diverging Soil Phosphorus Trajectories Across China's Croplands (1980–2018)
methods_radar.note: Applies harmonised national soil surveys and a machine-learning framework to map decades of cropland phosphorus change.

methods_radar.title: Predicting Coral Offspring Survival From Reef Thermal Histories in Ningaloo World Heritage Reef
methods_radar.note: Applies boosted regression trees to satellite-derived reef thermal histories to predict coral offspring survival.

methods_radar.title: Strategic dam management can offset climate‐driven declines in fish production
methods_radar.note: Applies a hierarchical Bayesian stock-recruitment model to link climate and dam flow management to trout productivity.

methods_radar.title: eDNA sampling reveals no evidence of negative effects of beaver recolonisation on the catchment‐scale distribution of migratory fish
methods_radar.note: Applies catchment-wide eDNA sampling to test beaver recolonisation effects on migratory fish distribution.

methods_radar.title: Effects of urban vegetation on the surface urban heat island: a UAV approach with a focus on private gardens
methods_radar.note: Applies UAV multispectral imagery and Random Forest classification to assess urban heat island mitigation.

methods_radar.title: Spatiotemporal dynamics and cross-boundary connectivity of an ecological network across a fragmented landscape
methods_radar.note: Applies Landsat time series, Random Forest and graph-based connectivity indices to quantify four decades of fragmentation.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: Applies multiscale species distribution models to quantify seascape niche overlap in native and invasive fish.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-satellite vegetation index datasets to compare forest growth between karst and non-karst nature reserves.

methods_radar.title: Farmland bird diversity requires heterogeneity between and within habitats
methods_radar.note: Applies passive acoustic monitoring with BirdNET classification to survey farmland bird diversity across mosaic landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Are forestry clearcuts ecological traps for caribou?
methods_radar.note: Applies step-selection functions to GPS-collared caribou to test clearcuts as ecological traps.

methods_radar.title: Quaternary Ice Ages Shaped Protists Phylogeography: The Case of Arcellinida in the Iberian Peninsula
methods_radar.note: Applies an extensive metabarcoding dataset to test the genetic-legacy-of-Quaternary paradigm in terrestrial protists.

methods_radar.title: Tropical forest restoration can offset water flux losses from deforestation but not everywhere
methods_radar.note: Applies satellite-derived evapotranspiration and precipitation observations to quantify forest restoration's hydrological offset potential.

methods_radar.title: Asymmetric transferability of trait–environment relationships between native and exotic plant species in Singaporean forests differing in land‐use history
methods_radar.note: Applies trait-based species distribution models to test transferability between native and exotic plants.
